preparation_method: assembled in corn or flour tortilla shell
topped with fresh vegetables and condiments

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 beef tacos, each approximately 110 g, totalling approximately 330 g, as commonly served at Mexican restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Taco, beef and cheese, hard shell — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 21340)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for beef and cheese hard-shell taco used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values represent a composite beef taco with standard toppings (lettuce, tomato, cheese, sour cream). Adapted to reflect a 3-piece restaurant serving context. Sodium adjusted upward slightly to reflect restaurant-level seasoning typical of Singapore-served Mexican food. All per-100g values derived from USDA data.

gi_source.title: Tortilla, corn — Glycemic Index Database, University of Sydney; composite GI for beef taco estimated from corn tortilla GI anchor
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry for beef taco exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 46 based on the corn tortilla GI anchor (GI ~46, low category, per University of Sydney GI Database) combined with the moderating effect of protein (beef ~10 g/100g) and fat (~11 g/100g), which lower the overall glycemic response of the composite dish. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (46 × 65.3 g carbohydrate in 330 g serving) / 100 ≈ 30.0; rounded to 28.9 after accounting for fibre adjustment. This is an analogous estimate and should be replaced with a direct measurement if available.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~390 mg/100g; ~1287 mg per 3-taco serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit beef taco consumption to one taco per sitting and request reduced-salt seasoning, as a 3-piece serving provides approximately 1287 mg sodium, representing over 55% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~28.9 per 3-taco serving) and refined carbohydrate from tortilla shells
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that a 3-piece beef taco serving carries a moderate glycemic load of approximately 29, and should consider choosing corn tortillas over flour tortillas, pairing with non-starchy vegetables, and monitoring post-meal blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.6 g/100g; ~15.2 g per serving) and sodium load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit beef taco intake due to the combined saturated fat (~15 g per 3-taco serving) and sodium (~1287 mg per serving) burden, and should prefer lean beef, reduced-fat cheese, and avocado-based toppings to improve the lipid profile of the meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Tacos (3 Pieces) is a Mexican dish consisting of three individual taco shells — typically made from corn or flour tortillas — filled with seasoned ground or sliced beef, and topped with a combination of shredded lettuce, diced tomato, grated cheese, sour cream, salsa, onion, and fresh cilantro. The beef filling is typically seasoned with a blend of cumin, chili powder, garlic, and paprika, then pan-fried or grilled until cooked through. The tortilla shell may be served hard (baked or fried corn shell) or soft (warm flour or corn tortilla), depending on the restaurant style. In Singapore, beef tacos are served at Mexican-themed restaurants and are a popular sharing or individual meal option. Each taco weighs approximately 110 g, and a 3-piece serving totals approximately 330 g. Nutritionally, a 3-piece serving provides approximately 719 kcal, 33.7 g protein, 37.6 g fat (including ~15 g saturated fat), and 65.3 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is notable at approximately 1287 mg per serving, which is relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 46 (low category), but the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~29) due to the carbohydrate volume from the tortilla shells. Beef tacos provide meaningful amounts of iron, zinc, vitamin B12, and calcium from the cheese component, making them a nutritionally dense meal when consumed in moderation.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 3 beef tacos?
How much protein in beef tacos?
Is beef taco good for diabetics?
Can I eat beef tacos with high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for beef and cheese hard-shell taco (NDB No. 21340), which is the closest approved-source match. No Singapore-specific or HPB Foodo entry for beef tacos was identified. GI is an analogous estimate based on corn tortilla GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium because the nutrient profile is well-supported by USDA data but the exact restaurant formulation (seasoning, tortilla type, topping ratios) at Fiesta Mexico / Three Amigos Singapore may differ from the USDA reference.
notes: 1. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for beef taco. 2. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for beef taco. 3. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for beef taco. 4. GI is an analogous estimate (GI=46) anchored to corn tortilla GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct beef taco GI measurement was found in approved sources. 5. Halal status is marked false as it cannot be confirmed without restaurant certification; the restaurants (Fiesta Mexico / Three Amigos) serve both Indian and Mexican food and halal status is unverified. 6. Serving size of 330 g (3 × ~110 g tacos) is estimated from standard restaurant portion norms; actual weight may vary. 7. Sodium value (~390 mg/100g) reflects a slight upward adaptation from USDA baseline to account for typical restaurant-level seasoning. 8. The cuisine_tag 'Naan' in the input record is noted as an artefact of the restaurant also serving Indian food; it is not relevant to this beef taco record and has been excluded from the output.
